name: Duryodhana
description: Duryodhana, the eldest of the hundred Kaurava brothers, is a central figure in the Hindu epic Mahabharata. Born to Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, he was characterized by immense strength, ambition, and a deep-seated jealousy of his cousins, the Pandavas. His birth was marked by inauspicious omens, and despite warnings, Dhritarashtra's blind love for his son led him to disregard advice to abandon him. Duryodhana's life was consumed by a relentless pursuit of power and a desire to usurp the Pandavas' rightful claim to the throne of Hastinapura. He orchestrated numerous schemes to eliminate them, including the infamous Lakshagriha (house of lac) incident and the disrobing of Draupadi. His arrogance and refusal to negotiate a peaceful resolution to the conflict ultimately led to the devastating Kurukshetra War, where he met his demise at the hands of Bhima. Despite his villainous portrayal, Duryodhana is also viewed by some as a complex character with admirable qualities such as courage, loyalty to his friends, and strong leadership. He is a symbol of ambition, envy, and the destructive consequences of unchecked desires. His unwavering commitment to his principles, even if misguided, makes him a compelling and controversial figure in Hindu mythology.
subtitle: The Ambitious Kaurava Prince
achievements: Mastered the art of wielding the mace
Became the crown prince of Hastinapura
Built a powerful alliance of kings
Displayed unwavering loyalty to his friends

keyMyths.title: The Lakshagriha Incident
keyMyths.description: Duryodhana, fueled by jealousy, plotted to burn the Pandavas alive in a house made of lac. However, the Pandavas escaped, foiling his initial attempt to seize the kingdom.

keyMyths.title: The Game of Dice
keyMyths.description: Duryodhana, with Shakuni's help, cheated Yudhishthira in a game of dice, leading to the Pandavas' exile and Draupadi's humiliation. This event significantly escalated the conflict.

keyMyths.title: Duryodhana's Defeat
keyMyths.description: In the Kurukshetra War, Duryodhana was defeated by Bhima in a mace fight. Krishna manipulated the rules, allowing Bhima to strike Duryodhana below the belt, fulfilling a past vow.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Duryodhana and the Kurukshetra War.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Duryodhana and the Kurukshetra War.
